mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL and FLT3 among other kinases; active in FLT3-ITD AML
additional_notes: Frequently single-digit to tens of nM in FLT3-ITD AML cells

mechanism: Inhibits MET, VEGFR2, and FLT3; reported FLT3 inhibition in AML
additional_notes: Low-nM activity reported in FLT3-ITD AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, VEGFR, and PDGFR family kinases
additional_notes: Typically tens to low hundreds of nM in FLT3-ITD AML
Often low- to mid-nM activity versus FLT3-ITD AML cells

mechanism: Inhibits SRC and ABL family kinases
additional_notes: Activity reported in subsets of AML lines, including MLL- or SRC-dependent contexts

mechanism: Allosteric inhibitor of MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway
additional_notes: RAS/MAPK-driven AML models are more responsive; may be cytostatic

mechanism: Pan-HDAC inhibitor causing histone acetylation and transcriptional changes
additional_notes: Broad AML potency commonly in low-nM
AML cell viability often inhibited at low-nM

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increasing oxidative stress
additional_notes: Reported sub- to low-µM activity in AML including FLT3-ITD models

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial protein synthesis by targeting mitoribosomes
additional_notes: Targets leukemia stem/OXPHOS dependence; low-µM in AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial ETC complex III to suppress OXPHOS
additional_notes: Low- to mid-µM growth inhibition in AML cells, including FLT3-ITD

mechanism: Induces ER stress and inhibits PI3K–AKT signaling
additional_notes: Typically low- to mid-µM in AML

mechanism: Disrupts Wnt/STAT3 signaling and mitochondrial OXPHOS
additional_notes: Often sub-µM in leukemia cells; watch solubility

mechanism: ALDH inhibition; copper-dependent proteasome inhibition and oxidative stress
additional_notes: AML activity commonly sub- to low-µM in vitro; copper can enhance effect

mechanism: Prodrug of teriflunomide; inhibits dihydroorotate dehydrogenase to block de novo pyrimidine synthesis
additional_notes: Differentiation/anti-proliferative effects; AML IC50s often in tens of µM

mechanism: Inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, blocking mevalonate pathway and prenylation
additional_notes: Statins show low-µM AML cytotoxicity via mevalonate-pathway dependence

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 to block JAK/STAT signaling
additional_notes: Stronger effects in JAK/STAT–dependent contexts; single-agent potency in MOLM-13 may be modest but useful in combinations
